title: Fix repeated production deployment failure

requirements.text: Investigate and resolve the recurring production deployment error. Audit the backend Motoko actor (backend/main.mo) for any syntax errors, type mismatches, or unstable type issues that would cause the canister build or upgrade to fail. Ensure the actor compiles cleanly and all stable variable declarations are valid.

quotes: Failed to deploy to production: Deployment error

acceptanceCriteria: The backend Motoko actor compiles without errors or warnings that block deployment.
All stable variables use only stable types (no mutable objects, closures, or non-stable types in stable declarations).
Production deployment completes successfully without a 'Deployment error'.

requirements.text: Audit the frontend build configuration (vite config, tsconfig, tailwind config, index.html, package.json) for any issues that would cause the production build to fail — such as missing environment variables, broken imports, type errors, or invalid asset references. Ensure the frontend builds cleanly for production.

quotes: Failed to deploy to production: Deployment error

acceptanceCriteria: The frontend production build completes without errors.
No broken imports, missing assets, or unresolved TypeScript type errors exist in the build output.
The deployed app loads correctly in production.

constraints: Do not change the application's existing features or user-facing behavior.
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Adding new features or UI changes.
Changing the backend data model or business logic.
Modifying authentication or access control logic beyond what is needed to fix the build.
